pages.raw_text:
Captain McClelland has arranged
for the grand opening of the Ondawa
inn tonight. The opening will be on
large scale with a big 1o-cent meal
10 start OF with for the winter sea-
son.


But there will be no union help to
serve the big meals for the small
price. The captain has taken de-
cided stand in this connection and
this winter will engage his help re-
gardless of union demands.


Last spring the boycott of the On
dawa inn by the union cooks and
walters created considerable excite
ment around the corner of Stevens
street and Front avenue. with arrests
and legal fights which ended In the
captain securing a permanent mjunc-
tion preventing the union pickets
from interfering with the inn busi-
ness.
